channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: South Korean president apologizes, saying he'll take responsibility for attempt at martial law
item.description: South Korea’s president apologized Saturday for public anxiety caused by his short-lived attempt to impose martial law earlier this week hours ahead of a parliamentary vote on impeaching him.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung And Hyung-Jin Kim, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Romania's top court annuls first round of presidential vote won by far-right candidate
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — A top Romanian court on Friday annulled the first round of the country's presidential election, days after allegations emerged that Russia ran a coordinated online campaign to promote the far-right outsider who won the first round.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: NBA returning to China more than five years after Daryl Morey Hong Kong tweet
item.description: The National Basketball Association (NBA) will return to China for two preseason games in 2025, more than five years after former Houston Rockets general manager Daryl Morey spurred a break in the relationship with an image supporting Hong Kong protesters.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: Notre Dame's reopening will begin with an archbishop's knock on the doors. Here's what comes next
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The reopening this weekend of Notre Dame is a succession of ceremonies to breathe life back into the iconic cathedral and celebrate the recovery from its devastating fire in 2019. Forecasts of stormy winds forced a late change in plans Friday, moving all of Saturday's events indoors.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Lebanon closes all its land border crossings with Syria except one
item.description: Lebanon is closing all its land border crossings with Syria except for a main one that links Beirut with the Syrian capital Damascus, the General Security Directorate said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mexico study's surprising finding: Killer heat hit harder for the young than the elderly
item.description: A surprising study of temperature-related deaths in Mexico upends conventional thinking about what age group is hit hardest by heat. Researchers found at higher temperatures and humidity, the heat kills far more young people under 35 than those older than 50.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Romania annuls first round of presidential vote won by far-right candidate
item.description: Romania's top court annulled the first round of the country's presidential election after intelligence was declassified alleging Russia interfered to promote Calin Georgescu.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran launches rocket with heaviest-ever payload into space amid heightened concern over nuclear program
item.description: Iran launches rocket into space carrying its heaviest-ever payload, as security experts remain concerned by its "drastically" increased stockpiles of enriched uranium near nuclear weapons grade purity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin signs agreement offering Russia's security guarantees to ally Belarus
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin visited Belarus on Friday to sign a treaty offering security guarantees for Moscow’s closest ally, including the possible use of Russian nuclear weapons to help repel an aggression.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Berlin's new panda twins have been named. Meet Leni and Lotti, or Meng Hao and Meng Tian
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Germany's new panda twins now have names — or rather, two names apiece. The two female cubs, born at the Berlin Zoo on Aug. 22, were introduced Friday as Meng Hao and Meng Tian ("beautiful dreams" and “sweet dreams”).
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ailing kids wait months for Israeli permission to leave Gaza for treatment. Some die in the meantime
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The 12-year-old Palestinian boy was lying in a hospital bed in central Gaza, wracked with leukemia, malnourished and whimpering in pain despite the morphine doctors were giving him, when Rosalia Bollen, a UNICEF official, said she saw him in late October.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Where Turkey stands as opposition insurgents advance in neighboring Syria
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — As forces opposed to Syrian President Bashar Assad make swift and significant advances in Syria, Turkey is watching closely for any spillover.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In the 6-hour mayhem of South Korea's martial law, a woman's act of resistance was a defining moment
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — In the groundswell of fury and defiance that erupted among South Koreans after their president declared martial law, curtailing the country's hard-won freedoms, it was perhaps the iconic moment.
item.dc:creator: YONG HO KIM and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thousands flee as Syrian insurgents advance to the doorstep of the country's third-largest city
item.description: Thousands of people have fled the central Syrian city of Homs, the country’s third largest, as insurgents seized two towns on the outskirts Friday.
item.dc:creator: Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Canada bans more assault firearms, suggests donating guns to Ukraine
item.description: Top leaders in Canada announced that they had banned an additional 324 assault-style firearms in a continued effort for more stringent gun control.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea's governing party head supports suspending Yoon's powers, making impeachment more likely
item.description: South Korea’s governing party chief is expressing support for suspending the constitutional powers of President Yoon Suk Yeol for imposing martial law, in a bombshell reversal that makes Yoon’s impeachment more likely.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Donald Trump's incoming team met with Ukraine officials
item.description: Trump's team is trying to bring Ukraine and Russia to the negotiating table quickly, potentially through diplomatic coercion.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean president's martial law declaration a 'blunder,' could embolden North Korea, expert says
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol declared martial law and now faces impeachment over the surprising move from opposition lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Voters want more US involvement on world stage despite isolationist talk, Ronald Reagan Institute survey finds
item.description: The Ronald Reagan Institute released its sixth annual National Defense Survey and found 'record high' support for U.S. involvement abroad despite a growing sense that isolationism could be on the rise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawmakers hold moment of silence for slain Omer Neutra as thousands mourn in hometown synagogue
item.description: A bipartisan group of lawmakers held a moment of silence for the slain Omer Neutra, an American-Israeli who was killed by Hamas on Oct. 7, 2023, according to new intelligence uncovered by the IDF.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawmakers see 'bad guys on both sides' in Syria offensive
item.description: Senators on both sides of the aisle are holding back from cheering rebel gains against Syrian President Bashar Assad, following a lightning offensive over the weekend led by Hayat Tahrir al-Sham (HTS), a U.S.-designated terrorist group.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: US 'seriously concerned' by developments in South Korea
item.description: The United States was surprised by South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol’s brief declaration of emergency martial law.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with Hungary’s effort to end Holocaust survivor lawsuit
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday probed the international consequences of allowing a group of Holocaust survivors and their heirs to leverage U.S. courts to seek compensation from Hungary for confiscating their property.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Chicago welcomes eight children from Gaza for medical treatment
item.description: Over the next few months, the children will receive medical treatment at U.S. hospitals, free of cost.
item.dc:creator: Emmy Victor, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean parliament votes to defy president by lifting his declaration of martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol declared martial law late Tuesday, vowing to eliminate “anti-state” forces as he struggles against an opposition that controls the country’s parliament and that he accuses of sympathizing with communist North Korea.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Guinea stadium stampede kills 56 following clashes at soccer match
item.description: Reports said security had tried to use tear gas to restore calm after the chaos that followed a disputed penalty call.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hong Kong launches panda sculpture tour as the city hopes the bear craze boosts tourism
item.description: Thousands of giant panda sculptures will greet residents and tourists starting Saturday in Hong Kong, where enthusiasm for the bears has grown since two cubs were born in a local theme park.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hamas releases new hostage video of American-Israeli Edan Alexander
item.description: Hamas released a propaganda video Saturday showing the 20-year-old Alexander alive, marking his first proof of life since being kidnapped.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation
